semanticCore: Stripping away, extracting, disputing, or tearing out
primaryMeaning: To pull out or strip away
quranicRelevance: Extracting souls violently (Naz' - 'wan-nazi'ati gharqan'), stripping away power or clothes, and dispute/dissension (Niza' - 'wa la tanaza'oo').

keyDerivations.meaning: To strip away, remove, pull out, extract, or take away
To dispute, argue, contend mutually, or pass around
Those who extract/pull out violently (angels of death)

figurativeOrRareUsages: يَتَنَازَعُونَ فِيهَا كَأْسًا (52:23): They will pass around/exchange among themselves a cup (friendly passing of cups in Paradise, from naza' meaning to pull/exchange).
وَنَزَعْنَا مَا فِي صُدُورِهِمْ: And We will strip/remove whatever was in their chests of resentment.
discardedNoise: Pulling a bow string in archery.
Obscure details of camel migration paths (نزيع).
summaryEn: The root spans extraction and dispute, portraying the violent extraction of souls, the stripping of power, and the friendly passing of cups in Paradise.
summaryAr: يدل هذا الجذر على النزع والنزاع، واصفاً نزع الأرواح المذل، وسلب الملك والنعمة، والتنازع المتبادل اللطيف لكؤوس الجنة.
rawText: أَنْزَعُ : see أَجْلَحُ. إِنْتَزَعَ 8 See 1. === اِنْتَزَعَ مِنْهُ حَقَّهُ He wrested from him his right, or due. === اِنْتَزَعَ حَدِيثَهُ: see اِقتضب. بِئْرٌ نَزُوعٌ [A deep well] i. q. جَرْورٌ. (A, voce جَرْورٌ.) تَنَازَعْنَا الحَدِيثَ 6 We discoursed together; one with another. (TA, art. هصر.) === تَنَازَعُوا الَّجَزَ بَيْنَهُمْ (K, art. رجز,) They recited verses, or poetry, of the metre termed رَجَز one with another; as also تَعَاطَوْهُ. (TK, art. رجز.) === تَنَازَعٌ The contending in altercation, disputing, or litigating, one with another: (K:) or تَنَازَعُوا they disagreed, one with another; held different ways or opinions. (Msb.) مَنْزَعُ بِئْرٍ [The bottom of a well; the place from which the water is drawn]. (TA, art. متح.) نَازَعَهُ الحَبْلَ 3 He contended with him in pulling the rope; syn. جَاذَبَهُ إِيَّاهُ. Hence, نازعه فى كَذَا ‡ He contended, disputed, or litigated, with him, respecting such a thing. (Mgh.) === نَازَعَهُ الكَلَامَ ‡ He disputed with him in, or respecting, words. (TA.) === نَازَعَتْنِى نَفْسِى إِلَى هَواهَا, inf. n. نِزَاعْ, My soul strove with me to incline me to love her. (TA.) See 1. نَزَعٌ Baldness on each side of the forehead: see جَلَحٌ; and غَمَمٌ. نَزَعَ إِلَى أَهْلِهِ 1 , (S, K,) aor. ﹻ, (S,) inf. n. نِزَاعٌ (S, K) and نُزُوعٌ and نَزَاعةٌ; (K:) and 🡻نَازَعَ; (K;) He yearned towards or for, longed for, or desired, his family. (S, * K, * TA, PS). === نَزَعْتُ إِلَيْهِ inf. n. نِزَاعٌ, I yearned towards, longed for, or desired, him or it; syn. خَنَفْتُ . (Ham, p. 429.) See an ex. voce خَفْضٌ. === Hence. نَزَعَ بِى إِلَيْهِ It (desire) invited me to it. (Har, p. 606.) === نَزَعَ إِلَيْهِ He inclined to it. (Har, p. 234.) === نَزَعَ إِلَى عِرْقٍ كَرِيمٍ [He inclined to a noble radical, or ancestral, or hereditary quality: and in like manner, لَئِيمٍ]: and نَزَعَ إِلَى أَعْرَاقِهِ and نَزَعَهَا [he inclined to his radical, or ancestral, or hereditary, qualities]: and أَعْرَاقُهُ نَزَعْتْ بِهِ [his radical, or ancestral, or hereditary, qualities inclined him]. (L, in TA.) === نَزَع It inclined by likeness. (Msb.) === نَزَعَ إِلَى أَبِيهِ (S, Msb, K,) فى الشَّبَهَ (S,) and نَزَعَ أَيَاهُ, (K,) He resembled his father: (Msb, K:) or inclined to his father in likeness; syn. ذَهَبَ (S:) or he took after his father; had a natural likeness to him. === نُزُوعٌ signifies Yearning; and natural inclining. ___ نَزَعَ and 🡻اِنْتَزَعَ He pulled, plucked, or drew, out, or up, or off; removed from his or its place; displaced. (S, Msb, K.) === نَزَعَ ثَوْبَهُ, (Mgh, in art. خلع,) and نَعْلَهُ, (Mgh and Msb in that art.,) He pulled off his garment, and his sandal. See, however, خَلَعَ. === نَزَعَ (Msb, TA,) aor. ﹻ, (TA,) inf. n. نَزْعٌ (Msb, TA,) He was at the point [or in the agony] of death; meaning, of having his soul drawn forth: (Msb:) he gave up his spirit; as also 🡻نَازَعَ, inf. n. نِزَاعٌ. (TA.) === نَزَعَ فِى القُوْسِ He drew the bow; (S, Msb, K;) i. e., its string; or he drew, or pulled, the string of the bow with the arrow. (TA.) ___ تَنْزِعُهُ شَعَرَةٌ بَيْضَآءُ, relating to a horse: see أَسْفَى. نَزَّاعٌ Dragging much, or forcibly: see Kur, lxx. 16. === العرْقُ نَزَّاعٌ (see Freytag's Arab. Prov., ii. 168) is probably similar to العِرْقُ دسَّاسٌ, and means The radical, or ancestral, or hereditary, quality is wont to return to its usual possessor: or it may mean, is wont to draw. نَزْعَةٌ A baldness in the side of the forehead. See صَدْمَةٌ. نُزَّعٌ is pl. of نَازِعٌ; as is also نُزُعٌ. (TA.) See an ex. in a verse cited بابٌ.
